rationale: GT's only concern still live in the final diff is whether the legacy top-level `audio` fallback in tts_preprocess still has a real producer given #4527 moved to codes.audio (the docstring-staleness comment appears already fixed in this diff, so it's unfindable by either candidate). Y directly engages this via its voxtral_tts.py:214 finding (mutual exclusivity, checked callers) plus adds a genuinely valid dead-code catch (assertions.py:56) and other well-evidenced minor points. X's two findings (global containment-fallback loosening with a concrete counterexample, and the near-vacuous min() guard) are sharp and correct but orthogonal to GT's actual concerns. Both candidates are precise and concretely actionable with file/line citations, but Y's broader, better-grounded coverage gives it the recall edge.
